research_question: After pausing the current price-volume family tuning line and deciding not to promote any round-5 keeper directly into a new family, which more standardized atomic price-volume signals inspired by Alpha158-style feature construction and common price-volume research can expand the retained keeper pool under the frozen v18 operational contract before any later family construction is reopened?

discovery_contract.builder_rule: each candidate builder may rank exactly one raw signal field and may not combine multiple raw signals inside the same candidate
discovery_contract.source_inspiration_rule: candidate definitions may draw inspiration from Alpha158-style price-volume expressions and common price-volume research papers, but all fields must be implemented independently inside this project's own data and scoring stack

signal_pool.interpretation: a stronger positive 20-day correlation between daily return and change in traded amount may encode a standardized price-volume confirmation signal

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This standardized Alpha-style label is economically the same price-volume confirmation mechanism and is retained only to document the naming bridge.
dedup_note.override_reason: Historical candidate id kept the Alpha-style alias label, but prereg field is normalized to the canonical signal name.

signal_pool.interpretation: a smaller short-vs-medium turnover deviation may capture cleaner participation normalization than simple turnover acceleration alone

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This used the same canonical turnover-acceleration mechanism but intentionally tested the opposite ranking direction under a standardized naming frame.
dedup_note.override_reason: Historical Alpha-style candidate was retained only as a preregistered inverse-direction test of the canonical turnover-acceleration field.

signal_pool.interpretation: a stronger recent amount momentum relative to a 20-day baseline may reveal standardized volume persistence

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This standardized volume-momentum label maps onto the same canonical amount-shock mechanism and is kept only for naming normalization.
dedup_note.override_reason: Historical candidate id preserved the Alpha-style alias label, but the field is normalized to the canonical amount-shock name.

signal_pool.interpretation: signals that persistently close nearer the top of the daily range may represent a standardized trend-quality signal

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This is a standardized label for the same intraday close-location mechanism already present in canonical form.
dedup_note.override_reason: Historical candidate id kept the standardized alias wording, while the prereg field is normalized to the canonical close-location name.

signal_pool.interpretation: lower recent upper-shadow pressure may indicate less overhead supply and cleaner continuation structure

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This standardized pressure wording maps onto the same upper-shadow candle-pressure mechanism already named canonically.
dedup_note.override_reason: Historical candidate id preserved the standardized alias, but the prereg field is normalized to the canonical upper-shadow ratio.

signal_pool.interpretation: a more efficient 60-day path may provide a slower and more standardized trend-quality edge than broad momentum alone

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This tests whether the canonical path-efficiency mechanism improves on a slower horizon under standardized naming.
dedup_note.override_reason: Registered as a 60-day horizon variant of the canonical path-efficiency mechanism.

signal_pool.interpretation: signals trading closer to the recent 20-day breakout boundary may encode a standardized structural-position signal

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This standardized breakout-distance wording maps onto the same canonical breakout-proximity mechanism.
dedup_note.override_reason: Historical candidate id preserved the standardized alias, but the prereg field is normalized to the canonical breakout-proximity name.

signal_pool.interpretation: signals trading farther from the recent 20-day breakdown boundary may encode downside-distance resilience

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This introduced a downside-distance structural resilience mechanism that was not already canonicalized elsewhere.

signal_pool.interpretation: lower recent range expansion relative to a 20-day baseline may capture a standardized compression state distinct from level volatility

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This introduced a standardized compression-state mechanism that is not merely a relabeling of volatility level.

signal_pool.interpretation: stronger recovery after recent weak opens or intraday downside pressure may encode a standardized resilience signal

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This introduced a standardized downside-recovery mechanism distinct from prior gap-recovery or close-location signals.

discovery_contract.aggregation_rule: none; this round explicitly forbids combining signals
discovery_contract.missing_data_rule: score nulls are allowed and must be preserved for audit; no imputation or cross-signal fallback is allowed

constants_frozen.selection_contract: same calibrated 0.70 ranking liquidity guard, same TopK=10 width, and same v18 top-15 refresh hysteresis
constants_frozen.portfolio_contract: same v18 refreshed-portfolio implementation until any later family round explicitly changes it

promotion_rule.phase_gate: Only atomic signals that show directionally coherent positive edge in signal-edge diagnostics may advance into a later composability-screening or family-construction round.
promotion_rule.non_promotion_rule: A signal that shows negative or near-zero broad ordering, inverted decile structure, or clearly weaker head labels than both rank11_20 and tail labels should be rejected rather than rescued by later extraction or overlay tweaks.

disallowed_changes: Do not mix multiple raw signals into one candidate inside this round.
Do not change the calibrated 0.70 ranking liquidity guard threshold or guard stage.
Do not change the frozen v18 refresh hysteresis rule.
Do not change benchmark or cost contracts.
Do not add extraction, weight-penalty, turnover-suppression, stability-cap, overlay, or family-composition changes inside this round.
Do not import or depend on the neighboring qlib data adapter layer; this project must implement all candidate fields independently on its own data contracts.
Do not introduce PIT quality, value, or investment fields into this round.
notes: This round starts after pausing the current price-volume family tuning line despite new round-5 keepers, because subsequent composability screens remained mixed and did not justify direct promotion.

The method now reopens atomic discovery with a more standardized candidate set inspired by Alpha158-style price-volume feature ideas and common price-volume research, while preserving this project's independent implementation boundary.
The objective is to discover cleaner, more reusable atomic signals before any later composability screen or family construction is reopened.
